Ocean Monitoring Indicator (OMI) of the Copernicus 
Marine Environment Monitoring Service 
SEAICE EXTENT INDICATOR 
ARCTIC_OMI_SI_extent 
ANTARCTIC_OMI_SI_extent  
 
 Issue: 2.1 
Contributors: Thomas Lavergne, Signe Aaboe, Cecilie Wettre 
Approval date by the CMEMS product quality coordination team: November 2025

I CHANGE RECORD 
When the quality of the products changes, the QuID  is updated and a row is added to this table.  The third 
column specifies which sections or sub-sections have been updated.  The fourth column should mention the 
version of the product to which the change applies. 
 
Issue Date § Description of Change Author Validated By

extracted from CMEMS -

Product name: OMI_SI_extent 
Dataset names: arctic_omi_si_extent, antarctic_omi_si_extent 
QUID for CMEMS OMIs: SIE Ref: 
Date: 
Issue: 
      OMI_SI_extent 
11/2025 
V2.1 
 
 
     Page 3/ 6 
II INTRODUCTION 
This document provides to CMEMS users information on the quality of the OMI monthly Sea Ice Extent (SIE) 
index for the Arctic and Antarctic.  
The data are monthly averaged SIE values. They are extracted and reformatted from the OSI SAF Sea Ice Index 
(SII, OSI-420) v2.2 product. See the PUM for a description of the CMEMS OMI product and its relation to the 
OSI SAF SII. 
SIE is an integrated quantity and no ground-truth is available for its validation. It is here compared to a similar 
indicator from the US National Snow and Ice Data Center (NSIDC): the NSIDC Sea Ice Index (SII) v3. 
 
QUID for CMEMS OMIs: SIE Ref: 
Date: 
Issue: 
      OMI_SI_extent 
11/2025 
V2.1 
 
 
     Page 4/ 6 
III SCIENTIFIC VALIDATION 
An evaluation of the monthly SIE values is documented in dedicated OSI SAF reports for the OSI SAF Sea Ice 
Index v2.2, accessible from https://osi-saf.eumetsat.int/products/osi-420 
In summary, the SIE values from the OSI SAF are similar but not identical to those of NSIDC (stemming from 
differences in the processing of SIC maps). The OSI SAF values are in general larger than those of NSIDC, with 
differences reaching 0.5 millions km2 in Northern Hemisphere summer. Average differences in the Southern 
Hemisphere are about 0.3 millions km2 in all months. The two time series agree in terms of SIE trends in both 
hemispheres, with the NSIDC data showing slightly larger relative trends than OSI SAF during summer, mostly 
due to the early period in the time series (1979-1987). 
The current OSI SAF sea ice index, version 2.3, is still in the demonstration stage, and preliminary quality 
assessments are available in the Product User Manual (PUM, version 1.3,  https://osi-
saf.eumetsat.int/products/osi-420). The official index product, version 3.0, is scheduled for review soon, with 
the final versions of the validation report, PUM, and the Algorithm Theoretical Baseline Document (ATBD) 
expected to be released in January 2026. Methodological details and comp lete validation results will be 
documented in these upcoming OSI SAF publications. 
QUID for CMEMS OMIs: SIE Ref: 
Date: 
Issue: 
      OMI_SI_extent 
11/2025 
V2.1 
 
 
     Page 5/ 6 
IV QUALITY CHANGES SINCE PREVIOUS VERSION 
This is the third version for the OMI SIE. 
The second version was based on the OSI SAF Sea Ice Index v2.1, that was itself based on the OSI SAF SIC CDR 
v2 (OSI-450 and OSI-430-b). 
The first version of the OMI had only coverage of the Northern Hemisphere, and was using an older version 
of the EUMETSAT OSI SAF sea-ice concentration data (OSI-409 and OSI-430). 
The new version of the OMI delivered in CMEMS is of higher quality (in accuracy, coverage, and robustness) 
than the previous versions. Compared to the second version, the new version is closer to the NSIDC values in 
the Northern Hemisphere.

catalog_entry.summary: This document describes the Ocean Monitoring Indicator (OMI) for Sea Ice Extent (SIE) in the Arctic and Antarctic, providing details on its quality, validation, and updates.

variables.long_name: Monthly averaged Sea Ice Extent (SIE) values
